title: Stenocalyx pistaciifolius
taxonomy: Stenocalyx pistaciifolius is a species of flowering plant in the family Burseraceae. It is native to Mexico and Central America. The classification of this plant is as follows: its Kingdom is Plantae, its Phylum is Angiospermophyta, its Class is Magnoliopsida, its Order is Sapindales, and its Family is Burseraceae. The genus Stenocalyx is part of the Burseraceae family. Further classification details may be found in the latest botanical publications and databases.

intro: Stenocalyx pistaciifolius, also known as Pistachio-leaf tree, is a plant species of the family Rutaceae, native to Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Stenocalyx pistaciifolius include limonoids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Stenocalyx pistaciifolius is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into salves and creams for topical application.
